_comment: rekit's OWN requirements (the dispatcher + the `rekit install` build path + a recommended analysis-workstation baseline). Distinct from per-skill prerequisites, which live in the central registry.json (each skill's `prerequisites`). Each tool uses the same check/min_version schema as a skill prereq, so the dispatcher's check_prereq() handles these unchanged. Per-platform `install` commands are PRINTED by `rekit setup` and never run automatically.

_tiers.base: rekit cannot run without these (dispatcher is python3 + stdlib; shim+build.sh are bash).
_tiers.build: needed by `rekit install` to vendor skill runtimes. Advisory — missing just means those skills can't be built, not that rekit is broken.
_tiers.recommended: agent/workstation niceties. No skill strictly requires these; they make a fresh box a good place to do RE/triage.

install.linux: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y python3 python3-pip
install.windows: # run inside WSL2, then: sudo apt install -y python3 python3-pip

base.why: the bin/rekit shim and every scripts/build.sh are bash

install.macos: # preinstalled (ships 3.2; fine). For a newer bash: brew install bash

install.linux: sudo apt install -y nodejs npm   # or use nvm for a current node
install.windows: # inside WSL2: sudo apt install -y nodejs npm   (or nvm)

build.why: vendoring pure-python deps via `uv pip --target` (the 4 binary analyzers, pyc-decompile, emulate-code, qiling-emulate). Only build-time.

install.windows: # inside WSL2: curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh   (native PS: irm https://astral.sh/uv/install.ps1 | iex)

recommended.why: fast content search; most agents prefer it over grep

install.windows: # inside WSL2: sudo apt install -y ripgrep
# inside WSL2: sudo apt install -y git
# inside WSL2: sudo apt install -y curl

recommended.why: parse the JSON result objects skills emit

install.windows: # inside WSL2: sudo apt install -y jq

recommended.why: quick format ID (bin-triage has its own, but `file` is handy at the shell)

install.macos: # ships with Xcode Command Line Tools: xcode-select --install
install.windows: # inside WSL2: sudo apt install -y file
